title: As playoffs near, doubt the 2025-26 Bruins at your own risk
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins, driven by young and experienced players, rallied to win 4-3 against the Columbus Blue Jackets.
Jeremy Swayman helped secure the victory with key saves, keeping Bruins' playoff hopes at nearly 92% according to MoneyPuck.
Boston's second line, featuring Pavel Zacha and Viktor Arvidsson, has outscored opponents 38-16 in 5-on-5 play.

title: Dating back to the 1600s, the second-oldest home in Cambridge lists for $5.8M
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Hooper-Lee-Nichols House, built circa 1685, is listed for $5.8 million in Cambridge.
The property has served as History Cambridge's headquarters since 1957 and has a preservation restriction.
The 6,449-square-foot home features historic elements including Parisian wallpaper and a Chandler Room library.

title: Jayson Tatum looked like a star again as Celtics roll past Hornets: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 32 points, leading a short-handed Celtics team to a 114-99 victory over the Hornets in Charlotte.
Payton Pritchard added 28 points and six assists, performing efficiently with 10-for-18 shooting as he returned to the starting lineup.
The Celtics' strategic defense targeted LaMelo Ball, contributing to a dominating win and potential playoff matchup insights against the Hornets.

title: Jaylen Brown boasts on social media after Celtics clinch playoff berth for 12th straight season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics clinch a playoff berth with a 114-99 victory over the Hornets, marking their 12th consecutive postseason appearance.
Despite injuries, Jayson Tatum scored a season-high 32 points, improving Celtics' record to 50-24, with 9-2 since his return.
Head Coach Joe Mazzulla credits organizational alignment for maintaining team's success despite roster changes over the last five years.

title: Connelly Early continues to shine for Red Sox after strong first start in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Connelly Early posted a 2.19 ERA in five career starts before the 2026 season opener against the Reds.
In a 3-2 loss to the Reds, Early pitched 5.1 innings, allowing one run and striking out six.
Early entered Boston's rotation due to roster adjustments, replacing Johan Oviedo and Ranger Suarez.

title: Boston Fleet cinch berth in Walter Cup Playoffs with win over Minnesota Frost
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet secured a Walter Cup Playoff spot with a 4-2 win over Minnesota Frost.
Fleet's record: 13-5-2-4; they're the fastest team to clinch playoff berth in league history.
Rookie Amanda Thiele saved 21 of 23 shots in her PWHL debut during the clinching game.

title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter after fatal stabbing in South Boston, police report.
Incident occurred Friday evening at 258 Old Colony Ave; victim died in hospital the next day.
Initial charges upgraded from assault to manslaughter; Pascual to be arraigned in South Boston District Court.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brockton police officers shot a 44-year-old man in the shoulder after he allegedly fired at them early Sunday.
A 71-year-old man sleeping in a nearby home was grazed by a bullet during the exchange of gunfire.
Both injured men were taken to hospitals with non-life threatening injuries; an investigation by State Police is underway.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
John Tortorella will replace Cassidy as coach of the Golden Knights, who are currently third in the Pacific Division.
The Golden Knights cited recent struggles and a need for change despite winning the Stanley Cup in 2023 under Cassidy.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots may select Clemson's T.J. Parker as their first-round pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Parker recorded 126 tackles, 21.5 sacks, and six forced fumbles over three seasons with Clemson.
New England seeks to enhance their pass rush after losing K'Lavon Chaisson and signing Dre'Mont Jones.
